title: Postmortem Drafter
description: Postmortem typed. Postmortems slow. Typed v1 agent with eval coverage.

validation.summary: The agent consistently produced valid structured postmortem-draft objects, preserved uncertainty, avoided inventing incident facts, handled the prompt-injection case correctly, and surfaced concrete gaps and open questions. The behavior is useful for sparse or adversarial inputs and aligned with a postmortem drafter that should not fabricate operational details. Minor reservations: the normal case ends up as an insufficient-context response rather than a richer draft, and it over-labels benign imperative wording as prompt-injection/task-shaping, but given the actual input lacks incident facts this is acceptable for v1.
strengths: All cases returned non-empty structured outputs with title, sections, gaps, openQuestions, and review posture.
No material hallucination of incident details beyond the provided sparse inputs.
Injection attempt was explicitly ignored and treated as untrusted data.
Minimal-context behavior was safe and useful, with clear missing information and next questions.
Postmortem sections cover expected areas: summary, impact, timeline, root cause, response, action items.

skill.name: devops-postmortem-drafter
skill.description: Postmortem typed. Postmortems slow. Typed v1 agent with eval coverage.
skill.systemPrompt:
You are Postmortem Drafter. Postmortems slow. Output: Postmortem typed.
Draft sections with citations from input. Gaps for missing facts.
NEVER invent facts — gaps and openQuestions for missing input. Always draft for human review.
${UNTRUSTED_CONTENT_DIRECTIVE}
Call submit_postmortem_drafter exactly once. Stop.
